publications.title: Thermally Driven Formation of Multiphase, Mixed-Dimensional Architectures from TaSe3 Nanoribbons
publications.authors: Rowe, C. F., Formo, E. V., Hachtel, J. A., and Salguero, T. T.
publications.abstract: We report on the thermally driven structural transformation of TaSe3 nanoribbons into complex mixed-dimensional architectures. Using in situ scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM) combined with heating, we observe real-time nucleation of new phases and structural reconstruction at the nanoscale.

publications.title: Achieving the 1D Atomic Chain Limit in Van der Waals Crystals
publications.authors: Teeter, J., Kim, N. Y., Debnath, T., Sesing, N., Geremew, T., Wright, D., Chi, M., Stieg, A. Z., Miao, J., Lake, R. K., Salguero, T. T., and Balandin, A. A.
publications.abstract: This work reports the liquid-phase exfoliation and electrical characterization of quasi-one-dimensional van der Waals crystals down to the individual atomic chain limit. High-resolution transmission electron microscopy and atomic force microscopy confirm the stability and structural integrity of the isolated chains, showing potential as ultra-scaled interconnects.

publications.title: One-dimensional van der Waals materials—Advent of a new research field
publications.authors: Balandin, A. A., Lake, R. K., and Salguero, T. T.
publications.abstract: An introduction to the special topic collection on the physics and materials science of one-dimensional van der Waals materials. We summarize recent breakthroughs in synthesis, device fabrication, and physical property modeling that characterize this rapidly developing field of materials chemistry.

publications.title: One-dimensional van der Waals quantum materials
publications.authors: Balandin, A. A., Kargar, F., Salguero, T. T., and Lake, R. K.
publications.abstract: This review provides an overview of the electronic, optical, phonon, thermal, and noise properties of quasi-one-dimensional van der Waals materials and their electronic and optoelectronic device applications. We outline the state of the art in the synthesis and exfoliation of these materials and highlight future research directions.

publications.abstract: Scientific research publication titled 'Quasi-1D van der Waals materials as high current-density local interconnects' published in NanoScience + Engineering (SPIE) (2016).

publications.title: Low-Frequency Electronic Noise in Quasi-1D TaSe3 van der Waals Nanowires
publications.authors: Liu, G., Rumyantsev, S., Bloodgood, M. A., Salguero, T. T., Shur, M., and Balandin, A. A.
publications.abstract: We report on low-frequency electronic excess noise in quasi-one-dimensional TaSe3 nanowires. The noise spectra show 1/f-like scaling at room temperature, which changes to Lorentzian-like GR noise peaks at lower temperatures. The current-carrying capacity is exceptionally high, pointing to their potential for nanoscale interconnect applications.

publications.title: A charge-density-wave oscillator based on an integrated tantalum disulfide–boron nitride–graphene device operating at room temperature
publications.authors: Liu, G., Debnath, B., Pope, T. R., Salguero, T. T., Lake, R. K., and Balandin, A. A.
publications.abstract: Transition-metal dichalcogenides exhibit a variety of collective electronic phases, including charge-density-wave (CDW) states. Here we demonstrate a room-temperature current-controlled oscillator operating via the CDW state in integrated devices consisting of thin-film 1T-TaS2 channels on hexagonal boron nitride with graphene gates. The devices exhibit high stability, low noise and high performance, showing prospects for applications in communications and signal processing.

publications.title: Nanoscience of an Ancient Pigment
publications.authors: Johnson-McDaniel, D., Barrett, C. A., Sharafi, A., and Salguero, T. T.
publications.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
publications.abstract: Egyptian blue (calcium copper tetrasilicate, CaCuSi4O10), first synthesized in ancient Egypt, is a layered silicate pigment. We report that Egyptian blue can be exfoliated into nanosheets of single-layer thickness by soft-chemical methods. These nanosheets possess robust near-infrared luminescence, opening applications in imaging, security inks, and planar solar concentrators.

publications.title: Decomposition of Ruthenium Olefin Metathesis Catalysts
publications.authors: Hong, S. H., Wenzel, A. G., Salguero, T. T., Day, M. W., and Grubbs, R. H.
publications.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
publications.abstract: Decomposition of ruthenium olefin metathesis catalysts (H2IMes)(PCy3)(Cl)2Ru=CHPh and (IMes)(PCy3)(Cl)2Ru=CHPh was investigated. In solution, these catalysts undergo primary decomposition via loss of the phosphine ligand, followed by coupling of the ruthenium-bound benzylidene ligand with the methyl group of the N-heterocyclic carbene ligand.
